# CHOOSER . which chart carries your message?

Built entries only; the full planned map is TAXONOMY.md.
Agents: match your message to an intent, check `see instead`
before committing, then render `<code> in DL-xx`.

## Intent: bridge

### CH-FLO-01 WATERFALL (waterfall / bridge)
- Data: a start total + signed delta steps; end total is computed, never supplied
- Use when: explaining how a total moved from A to B through named drivers
- Not when: independent variances with no running total (use CH-DEV-01)
- See instead: CH-DEV-01 when deltas are independent, not sequential
- See instead: CH-PTW-01 when composition of a static total
- Alternatives: CH-DEV-01 (diverging bar, when steps are independent)

## Intent: change-over-time

### CH-COR-04 COLUMN + LINE (columns on a left scale, one line on a labeled right scale)
- Data: ordered periods + one volume measure + one rate measure
- Use when: a volume and a rate that move together over time
- Not when: two arbitrary series dual-axed to force a story
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when both measures share a unit
- See instead: CH-COR-01 when the relationship, not time, is the question
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-01 (same units: two lines), CH-COR-01 (relationship itself)

### CH-DEV-02 DUMBBELL (two dots + connector per category: before and after)
- Data: categories with a value at two moments; the movement is the message
- Use when: before/after per category: backlog W24 vs W28, score last year vs now
- Not when: more than two moments (CH-TIM-01)
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when more than 2 time points
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-05 (few categories, slope reads better)

### CH-DEV-04 SURPLUS/DEFICIT AREA (one line over time with the area to zero filled by sign)
- Data: ordered periods + one signed series (balance, variance vs plan)
- Use when: balances that swing between surplus and deficit
- Not when: all-positive series (CH-TIM-03)
- See instead: CH-TIM-03 when the series never crosses zero
- See instead: CH-FLO-01 when explaining one period's change by driver
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-01 (no sign story), CH-FLO-01 (period deltas as a bridge)

### CH-PTW-01 STACKED BAR (vertical stacked bar)
- Data: ordered categories + 2 to 4 additive segments per category
- Use when: how a total splits, tracked across periods or categories
- Not when: shares only (use CH-PTW-02, 100% stacked)
- See instead: CH-PTW-02 when shares matter more than totals
- See instead: CH-MAG-01 when only one measure per category
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-02 (100% stacked), CH-PTW-05 (treemap, planned)

### CH-PTW-02 STACKED BAR 100% (vertical 100% stacked bar)
- Data: ordered categories + 2 to 4 additive segments; totals normalized away
- Use when: how the MIX shifts across periods or categories
- Not when: the total matters (use CH-PTW-01; 100% hides growth and shrinkage)
- See instead: CH-PTW-01 when the total itself matters
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-01 (absolute stacked), CH-PTW-06 (waffle, planned)

### CH-RNK-04 BUMP (rank positions connected across ordered periods)
- Data: 3-8 competitors with a full rank (1..n) per period
- Use when: rank order changes over time: who overtook whom
- Not when: values matter more than positions (CH-TIM-01)
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when the metric value itself is the story
- See instead: CH-TIM-05 when only two periods
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-01 (values matter more than positions)

### CH-TIM-01 LINE (line)
- Data: ordered periods + 2 to 4 numeric series
- Use when: trend over ordered periods
- Not when: categorical comparison (use CH-RNK-01 or CH-MAG-01)
- See instead: CH-TIM-02 when values are cumulative progress vs plan
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when comparison across categories
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-03 (area, planned), CH-TIM-05 (slope, planned), CH-TIM-06 (sparkline, planned)

### CH-TIM-02 S-CURVE (line (cumulative S-curve))
- Data: ordered periods + cumulative non-decreasing series (Actual key; Plan/Forecast context)
- Use when: cumulative progress vs plan over the project timeline
- Not when: non-cumulative period values (use CH-TIM-01 LINE)
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when values are per-period, not cumulative
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-08 (fan with uncertainty, planned)

### CH-TIM-03 AREA (filled area (single series))
- Data: ordered periods + one non-negative quantity series (optional context line)
- Use when: one quantity over time where the VOLUME matters (output, throughput, stock)
- Not when: comparing several series (use CH-TIM-01; overlapping areas lie)
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when 2+ series compared
- See instead: CH-TIM-02 when cumulative progress vs plan
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-01 (line), CH-TIM-04 (stacked area, planned)

### CH-TIM-04 STACKED AREA (stacked filled bands over time, bottom-up)
- Data: ordered periods + 2-4 non-negative component series summing to a meaningful total
- Use when: how a growing total splits into components over time
- Not when: reading exact middle-band values (baselines wander; CH-TIM-01)
- See instead: CH-TIM-03 when single quantity
- See instead: CH-PTW-02 when shares matter more than levels
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when series cross and levels must be read exactly
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-03 (one series), CH-PTW-01 (few periods)

### CH-TIM-05 SLOPE (two-point lines between two periods)
- Data: categories with a value at exactly two periods; direction and crossing are the message
- Use when: year-over-year or before/after across categories
- Not when: more than two periods (CH-TIM-01)
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when more than two periods
- Alternatives: CH-DEV-02 (horizontal reading)

### CH-TIM-07 CALENDAR HEATMAP (weekday x week grid, shaded and numbered)
- Data: a count or measure per weekday per week; the weekly rhythm is the message
- Use when: workload rhythm: deliveries, tickets, tests per weekday across weeks
- Not when: non-calendar dimensions (CH-COR-03)
- See instead: CH-COR-03 when dimensions are not calendar units
- Alternatives: CH-COR-03 (generic two-dimension grids)

### CH-TIM-10 CONNECTED SCATTER (scatter with points connected in time order)
- Data: one entity, two measures, several periods; the trajectory is the message
- Use when: trajectories: cost vs output, price vs volume, risk vs progress over years
- Not when: many entities (CH-COR-01)
- See instead: CH-COR-01 when many entities at one moment
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-01 (one measure over time)

### CH-TIM-13 STEP (step line (values hold between changes))
- Data: ordered periods + series whose value HOLDS until it changes (rates, stock, policy)
- Use when: levels that hold between discrete changes: stock, rates, headcount, policies
- Not when: continuously varying measures (use CH-TIM-01)
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when values change continuously
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-01 (continuous change)

### CH-TIM-14 INDEXED LINE (line, all series rebased to 100)
- Data: ordered periods + 2-4 series in DIFFERENT units or magnitudes; rebased to first period = 100
- Use when: comparing growth across series with different units or magnitudes
- Not when: absolute levels matter (rebasing hides them; use CH-TIM-01)
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when series share one unit and scale
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-01 (same-unit series)

## Intent: comparison

### CH-DEV-01 DIVERGING BAR (horizontal diverging bar from a zero axis)
- Data: one categorical dimension + one signed measure (variance vs plan, budget, or zero)
- Use when: signed variance per category: ahead/behind, over/under, surplus/deficit
- Not when: sequential deltas that bridge two totals (use CH-FLO-01)
- See instead: CH-FLO-01 when deltas chain into a running total
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when all values share one sign
- Alternatives: CH-DEV-02 (dumbbell, planned), CH-DEV-03 (spine, planned)

### CH-DEV-02 DUMBBELL (two dots + connector per category: before and after)
- Data: categories with a value at two moments; the movement is the message
- Use when: before/after per category: backlog W24 vs W28, score last year vs now
- Not when: more than two moments (CH-TIM-01)
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when more than 2 time points
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-05 (few categories, slope reads better)

### CH-DEV-03 SPINE (one bar per category split into two parts around a center axis)
- Data: categories with two complementary parts (done/remaining, yes/no)
- Use when: two-part splits compared across categories
- Not when: more than two parts (CH-PTW-02)
- See instead: CH-PTW-02 when 3 or more parts per category
- See instead: CH-DEV-05 when graded agreement levels
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-02 (100% stacked for 3-4 parts), CH-DEV-05 (agreement scales with 4 levels)

### CH-DST-02 DOT PLOT (two dots per category (two measures) on one scale)
- Data: categories with two comparable measures (planned vs actual, min vs max)
- Use when: two measures per category on one scale: planned vs actual, this vs that
- Not when: before/after change (CH-DEV-02 reads direction)
- See instead: CH-DEV-02 when the two values are before/after
- Alternatives: CH-DEV-02 (change over time reading)

### CH-DST-03 DOT STRIP (every raw value as a dot on one shared scale, one strip per category)
- Data: 3+ raw values per category, few enough to plot individually (<= ~30 per row)
- Use when: small samples where every raw value can be shown honestly
- Not when: hundreds of points per row (CH-DST-04 summarizes)
- See instead: CH-DST-07 when one pooled group, outliers by name
- See instead: CH-DST-04 when too many points to draw raw
- Alternatives: CH-DST-07 (one pooled swarm), CH-DST-04 (5-number summary)

### CH-DST-04 BOXPLOT (five-number summary per category: whiskers, quartile box, median tick)
- Data: categories with a declared five-number summary (min, q1, median, q3, max)
- Use when: comparing many distributions compactly
- Not when: general audiences (boxplots are widely misread; CH-DST-03)
- See instead: CH-DST-03 when few enough values to show raw
- See instead: CH-DST-03 when the audience reads quartiles poorly
- Alternatives: CH-DST-03 (few raw values), CH-DST-08 (shape beyond quartiles, warn)

### CH-DST-05 POPULATION PYRAMID (two mirrored bar stacks around a labeled center gutter, one row per band)
- Data: ordered bands (age, tenure, size class) with counts for two groups
- Use when: two groups across ordered bands: age structures, tenure mixes
- Not when: more than two groups (small multiples)
- See instead: CH-MAG-03 when categories have no natural band order
- See instead: CH-DEV-03 when the two parts share one whole
- Alternatives: CH-MAG-03 (unordered categories), CH-DEV-03 (two-part shares)

### CH-DST-10 ERROR BARS (dot with low-high interval per category)
- Data: categories with a central value and a low-high range (spread, CI, min-max)
- Use when: estimates that carry a range: durations, forecasts, measurement spread
- Not when: ranges are negligible (use CH-RNK-01)
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when only the central value matters
- Alternatives: CH-DST-04 (full distribution shape, planned)

### CH-MAG-01 COLUMN (vertical column)
- Data: one ordered categorical dimension + one non-negative measure
- Use when: comparing magnitude across categories that carry a natural order (sites, phases, units)
- Not when: the message is rank (use CH-RNK-01 BAR-H, sorted)
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when the message is rank
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when the data is a time series
- Alternatives: CH-MAG-04 (bullet, planned), CH-MAG-02 (paired column, planned)

### CH-MAG-02 PAIRED COLUMN (two columns per category, side by side)
- Data: ordered categories + exactly two comparable series (budget vs actual)
- Use when: two measures per category: budget vs actual, plan vs forecast
- Not when: more than two series (unreadable; use lines or small multiples)
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when more than two series
- Alternatives: CH-DST-02 (dots when columns crowd)

### CH-MAG-03 PAIRED BAR (two horizontal bars per category on one shared scale)
- Data: categories in a natural order with two comparable measures each
- Use when: two measures per category with long labels (plan vs actual, A vs B)
- Not when: ranking is the message (sort and use CH-RNK-01)
- See instead: CH-DST-02 when the gap between the two measures is the story
- See instead: CH-MAG-02 when labels are short and periods few
- Alternatives: CH-MAG-02 (vertical pairs for short labels), CH-DST-02 (dots when the gap is the story)

### CH-MAG-05 PROPORTIONAL SYMBOL (one circle per category, AREA proportional to value)
- Data: 2-7 categories with positive values of very different magnitude
- Use when: magnitudes spanning orders where bars would dwarf the small ones
- Not when: precise comparison (areas are read poorly; values must be printed)
- See instead: CH-MAG-01 when values are within one order of magnitude
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when precise reading matters
- Alternatives: CH-RNK-01 (precise comparison), CH-MAG-01 (similar magnitudes)

### CH-MAG-06 PICTOGRAM (rows of unit squares, each square a declared count)
- Data: 2-6 categories with counts; a declared unit value per square
- Use when: countable things told in countable marks (people, lots, trucks)
- Not when: non-count measures (rates, currency)
- See instead: CH-PTW-06 when the share of a whole is the message
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when no countable-units story
- Alternatives: CH-RNK-01 (no unit metaphor needed), CH-PTW-06 (share of 100)

### CH-MAG-07 RADAR (3-6 shared-unit axes from a center; one polygon per series)
- Data: 3-6 dimensions ON THE SAME SCALE for at most 2 series
- Use when: WARN: only when every axis shares one unit and the audience expects a radar
- Not when: different units per axis (the polygon area means nothing)
- See instead: CH-MAG-02 when axes have different units
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when one series only
- Alternatives: CH-MAG-02 (paired bars read better), CH-RNK-01 (single series)

### CH-RNK-01 BAR-H (horizontal bar)
- Data: one categorical dimension + one non-negative measure
- Use when: ranking categories by a single measure
- Not when: time series (use CH-TIM-01 LINE)
- See instead: CH-MAG-01 when natural category order matters
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when the data is a time series
- Alternatives: CH-RNK-03 (lollipop, planned), CH-RNK-06 (table with bars, planned)

### CH-RNK-02 ORDERED COLUMN (vertical column, sorted descending)
- Data: one categorical dimension + one non-negative measure, short names
- Use when: ranking with short category names where vertical reads naturally
- Not when: long names (CH-RNK-01)
- See instead: CH-MAG-01 when natural order matters
- Alternatives: CH-RNK-01 (long names)

### CH-RNK-03 LOLLIPOP (stick + dot, sorted descending)
- Data: one categorical dimension + one non-negative measure; lighter ink than bars
- Use when: ranking where bars would overwhelm the page (many rows, light values)
- Not when: values very close together (dots overlap visually less than bars separate)
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when many close values
- Alternatives: CH-RNK-01 (denser data)

### CH-RNK-05 DOT STRIP (one dot per category on a shared scale)
- Data: one categorical dimension + one measure; minimal ink
- Use when: compact ranking with minimal ink
- Not when: readers need the zero anchor (CH-RNK-03)
- See instead: CH-RNK-03 when the zero anchor matters
- Alternatives: CH-RNK-03 (adds the zero anchor)

### CH-SPA-02 PROPORTIONAL SYMBOL MAP (area-true circles at supplied coordinates over a base map)
- Data: base polygons + places [{name, at, value}]; absolute counts
- Use when: absolute counts anchored to places (stock, headcount, backlog)
- Not when: rates (symbol size reads as amount)
- See instead: CH-SPA-01 when rates or intensities
- See instead: CH-MAG-05 when geography adds nothing
- Alternatives: CH-SPA-01 (rates), CH-MAG-05 (no geography)

### CH-TAB-02 HEATMAP TABLE (entities x metrics table, cells shaded per column and numbered)
- Data: entity rows x numeric metric columns with different scales
- Use when: entities compared across metrics with different scales (progress %, punch count, tests left)
- Not when: one shared scale across all columns (use CH-COR-03's global mode)
- See instead: CH-TAB-01 when cells are statuses, not numbers
- See instead: CH-COR-03 when both dimensions are ordered
- Alternatives: CH-TAB-01 (status enums instead of numbers), CH-COR-03 (two ordered dimensions)

### CH-TIM-05 SLOPE (two-point lines between two periods)
- Data: categories with a value at exactly two periods; direction and crossing are the message
- Use when: year-over-year or before/after across categories
- Not when: more than two periods (CH-TIM-01)
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when more than two periods
- Alternatives: CH-DEV-02 (horizontal reading)

### CH-TIM-14 INDEXED LINE (line, all series rebased to 100)
- Data: ordered periods + 2-4 series in DIFFERENT units or magnitudes; rebased to first period = 100
- Use when: comparing growth across series with different units or magnitudes
- Not when: absolute levels matter (rebasing hides them; use CH-TIM-01)
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when series share one unit and scale
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-01 (same-unit series)

## Intent: composition

### CH-DEV-05 DIVERGING STACKED (4-level agreement bars diverging from a center axis)
- Data: statements with counts or shares across 4 graded levels (2 negative, 2 positive)
- Use when: survey scales: agreement, satisfaction, support
- Not when: scales with a neutral middle level (fold it out first and say so)
- See instead: CH-PTW-02 when levels have no negative/positive split
- See instead: CH-DEV-03 when only two parts per row
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-02 (no natural center), CH-DEV-03 (two parts only)

### CH-FLO-02 SANKEY (column-stacked nodes joined by width-true bands)
- Data: 2-3 node columns + links between adjacent columns; conserved totals
- Use when: where things came from and where they went, conserved end to end
- Not when: cyclic flows (sankeys are acyclic by construction)
- See instead: CH-FLO-01 when one quantity bridges start to end
- See instead: CH-PTW-01 when no movement between states
- Alternatives: CH-FLO-01 (one total changing through steps), CH-PTW-01 (composition without flow)

### CH-MAG-08 PARLIAMENT (semicircle of seats, one dot per unit, grouped contiguously)
- Data: 2-6 groups whose integer counts sum to a meaningful total of seats or slots
- Use when: seats, slots, berths: integer allocations where each unit matters
- Not when: non-integer measures
- See instead: CH-PTW-01 when values are not countable seats
- See instead: CH-PTW-06 when a share of 100
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-01 (shares without the seat metaphor), CH-PTW-06 (percent of 100)

### CH-PTW-01 STACKED BAR (vertical stacked bar)
- Data: ordered categories + 2 to 4 additive segments per category
- Use when: how a total splits, tracked across periods or categories
- Not when: shares only (use CH-PTW-02, 100% stacked)
- See instead: CH-PTW-02 when shares matter more than totals
- See instead: CH-MAG-01 when only one measure per category
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-02 (100% stacked), CH-PTW-05 (treemap, planned)

### CH-PTW-05 TREEMAP (squarified treemap, one level)
- Data: 4 to 12 additive categories of one total, one optional highlight
- Use when: a total split across 4 to 12 parts where the big picture beats precision
- Not when: precise comparisons (area is read badly; use CH-RNK-01)
- See instead: CH-PTW-03 when under 4 categories
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when precise comparison matters
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-01 (stacked bar), CH-RNK-01 (when order matters more than area)

### CH-PTW-07 MARIMEKKO (100% stacked columns whose widths carry a second total)
- Data: 2-6 categories with a total each, split into 2-4 shared segments
- Use when: two composition questions at once: how big is each category, and how does each split
- Not when: audiences reading it for the first time without a walkthrough
- See instead: CH-PTW-02 when column totals are close to equal
- See instead: CH-PTW-01 when only one dimension splits
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-02 (equal widths), CH-PTW-05 (one level, area only)

### CH-PTW-09 SUNBURST (two concentric rings: groups inside, children outside)
- Data: a strict 2-level hierarchy: 2-4 groups, 2-4 children each
- Use when: WARN: only when a 2-level whole must be shown in one figure
- Not when: angle comparison across rings (humans cannot)
- See instead: CH-PTW-05 when one level of hierarchy
- See instead: CH-PTW-02 when shares must be compared precisely
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-05 (one level, honest areas), CH-PTW-01 (few parts, exact)

### CH-TIM-04 STACKED AREA (stacked filled bands over time, bottom-up)
- Data: ordered periods + 2-4 non-negative component series summing to a meaningful total
- Use when: how a growing total splits into components over time
- Not when: reading exact middle-band values (baselines wander; CH-TIM-01)
- See instead: CH-TIM-03 when single quantity
- See instead: CH-PTW-02 when shares matter more than levels
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when series cross and levels must be read exactly
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-03 (one series), CH-PTW-01 (few periods)

### CH-TIM-11 STREAMGRAPH (stacked bands around a centered silhouette baseline)
- Data: ordered periods + 3-4 non-negative components; shape over readability
- Use when: WARN: editorial texture pieces where the organic silhouette IS the message
- Not when: any reading task at all: every baseline wanders by construction
- See instead: CH-TIM-04 when any value must be read
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when levels matter
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-04 (readable baselines), CH-TIM-01 (exact levels)

## Intent: correlation

### CH-COR-01 SCATTER (scatter plot, labeled points)
- Data: entities with two numeric measures; under ~20 points if labeled
- Use when: how two measures relate across entities
- Not when: time is one of the axes (use CH-TIM-01)
- See instead: CH-COR-02 when a third measure matters
- See instead: CH-COR-03 when hundreds of points
- Alternatives: CH-COR-02 (bubble, adds a size measure), CH-COR-03 (XY heatmap for dense grids)

### CH-COR-02 BUBBLE (bubble chart: scatter plus an area-coded third measure)
- Data: entities with two positional measures + one size measure; under ~15 points
- Use when: two related measures plus a weight (budget, volume, headcount)
- Not when: the size measure is decorative (use CH-COR-01)
- See instead: CH-COR-01 when the third measure adds nothing to the story
- Alternatives: CH-COR-01 (drop the size measure)

### CH-COR-03 XY HEATMAP (two ordered dimensions x one measure, cells shaded and numbered)
- Data: grid of values over two ordered dimensions (weekday x week, hour x day)
- Use when: patterns across two ordered dimensions: weekday x week, hour x day, section x month
- Not when: entity rows with mixed-scale metrics (use CH-TAB-02, column-normalized)
- See instead: CH-TAB-02 when rows are entities, not an ordered dimension
- See instead: CH-COR-01 when few points
- Alternatives: CH-TAB-02 (heatmap table for entity rows), CH-TIM-07 (calendar heatmap, planned)

### CH-COR-04 COLUMN + LINE (columns on a left scale, one line on a labeled right scale)
- Data: ordered periods + one volume measure + one rate measure
- Use when: a volume and a rate that move together over time
- Not when: two arbitrary series dual-axed to force a story
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when both measures share a unit
- See instead: CH-COR-01 when the relationship, not time, is the question
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-01 (same units: two lines), CH-COR-01 (relationship itself)

### CH-COR-05 HEXBIN (hexagonal bins colored by point count)
- Data: 50+ x,y points, too many for honest individual dots
- Use when: hundreds of points where overplotting hides the density
- Not when: few points (bins hide what dots would show)
- See instead: CH-COR-01 when under ~50 points
- See instead: CH-COR-03 when axes are categories, not measures
- Alternatives: CH-COR-01 (few enough points to draw raw), CH-COR-03 (categorical axes)

### CH-COR-06 PARALLEL COORDINATES (one polyline per item across 3-5 vertical zero-based axes)
- Data: 6-12 items measured on 3-5 dimensions; one item highlighted
- Use when: WARN: analyst-only views for spotting multi-dimensional profiles
- Not when: general audiences (line crossings read as meaning; they are layout)
- See instead: CH-COR-01 when two dimensions suffice
- See instead: CH-TAB-02 when exact per-cell reading
- Alternatives: CH-COR-01 (two dimensions), CH-TAB-02 (exact values per cell)

### CH-COR-07 SCATTERPLOT MATRIX (3x3 grid of mini scatters, every variable against every other)
- Data: exactly 3 measures over up to 30 items; overview before analysis
- Use when: WARN: analyst overview to pick which pairwise plot deserves a real chart
- Not when: presenting to any non-analyst (36 tiny axes explain nothing)
- See instead: CH-COR-01 when one pair is already known to matter
- See instead: CH-COR-05 when hundreds of points
- Alternatives: CH-COR-01 (the one pair that matters), CH-COR-05 (many points, one pair)

### CH-FLO-03 NETWORK (nodes at supplied positions, edges weighted by strength)
- Data: nodes [{id, at, value}] + edges [{a, b, weight}]; positions are data
- Use when: dependency and connection structures: who touches whom
- Not when: force-directed layouts (renderer randomness breaks determinism)
- See instead: CH-FLO-02 when flows have direction and volume
- See instead: CH-TAB-02 when exact pairwise values
- Alternatives: CH-FLO-02 (directional volumes), CH-TAB-02 (exact adjacency)

### CH-TIM-10 CONNECTED SCATTER (scatter with points connected in time order)
- Data: one entity, two measures, several periods; the trajectory is the message
- Use when: trajectories: cost vs output, price vs volume, risk vs progress over years
- Not when: many entities (CH-COR-01)
- See instead: CH-COR-01 when many entities at one moment
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-01 (one measure over time)

## Intent: density

### CH-COR-03 XY HEATMAP (two ordered dimensions x one measure, cells shaded and numbered)
- Data: grid of values over two ordered dimensions (weekday x week, hour x day)
- Use when: patterns across two ordered dimensions: weekday x week, hour x day, section x month
- Not when: entity rows with mixed-scale metrics (use CH-TAB-02, column-normalized)
- See instead: CH-TAB-02 when rows are entities, not an ordered dimension
- See instead: CH-COR-01 when few points
- Alternatives: CH-TAB-02 (heatmap table for entity rows), CH-TIM-07 (calendar heatmap, planned)

### CH-COR-05 HEXBIN (hexagonal bins colored by point count)
- Data: 50+ x,y points, too many for honest individual dots
- Use when: hundreds of points where overplotting hides the density
- Not when: few points (bins hide what dots would show)
- See instead: CH-COR-01 when under ~50 points
- See instead: CH-COR-03 when axes are categories, not measures
- Alternatives: CH-COR-01 (few enough points to draw raw), CH-COR-03 (categorical axes)

### CH-DST-09 DENSITY CURVE (one smooth declared density curve, filled to zero)
- Data: a DECLARED density curve: grid points + density shares, bandwidth stated
- Use when: one distribution's shape for a reader who knows what a density is
- Not when: audiences who read areas as counts (CH-DST-01)
- See instead: CH-DST-01 when bins are honest enough
- See instead: CH-DST-08 when comparing shapes across categories
- Alternatives: CH-DST-01 (honest bins, no smoothing), CH-DST-08 (mirrored, per category)

### CH-SPA-04 DOT DENSITY (one dot per declared count at supplied coordinates)
- Data: base polygons + dot coordinates; each dot a declared unit
- Use when: spatial concentration where the cluster shape matters
- Not when: few items (plot them as labeled symbols)
- See instead: CH-SPA-01 when region-level rates suffice
- See instead: CH-COR-05 when no geography
- Alternatives: CH-SPA-01 (rates by region), CH-COR-05 (abstract xy density)

### CH-TIM-07 CALENDAR HEATMAP (weekday x week grid, shaded and numbered)
- Data: a count or measure per weekday per week; the weekly rhythm is the message
- Use when: workload rhythm: deliveries, tickets, tests per weekday across weeks
- Not when: non-calendar dimensions (CH-COR-03)
- See instead: CH-COR-03 when dimensions are not calendar units
- Alternatives: CH-COR-03 (generic two-dimension grids)

## Intent: deviation

### CH-DEV-01 DIVERGING BAR (horizontal diverging bar from a zero axis)
- Data: one categorical dimension + one signed measure (variance vs plan, budget, or zero)
- Use when: signed variance per category: ahead/behind, over/under, surplus/deficit
- Not when: sequential deltas that bridge two totals (use CH-FLO-01)
- See instead: CH-FLO-01 when deltas chain into a running total
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when all values share one sign
- Alternatives: CH-DEV-02 (dumbbell, planned), CH-DEV-03 (spine, planned)

### CH-DEV-02 DUMBBELL (two dots + connector per category: before and after)
- Data: categories with a value at two moments; the movement is the message
- Use when: before/after per category: backlog W24 vs W28, score last year vs now
- Not when: more than two moments (CH-TIM-01)
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when more than 2 time points
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-05 (few categories, slope reads better)

### CH-DEV-04 SURPLUS/DEFICIT AREA (one line over time with the area to zero filled by sign)
- Data: ordered periods + one signed series (balance, variance vs plan)
- Use when: balances that swing between surplus and deficit
- Not when: all-positive series (CH-TIM-03)
- See instead: CH-TIM-03 when the series never crosses zero
- See instead: CH-FLO-01 when explaining one period's change by driver
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-01 (no sign story), CH-FLO-01 (period deltas as a bridge)

### CH-DEV-05 DIVERGING STACKED (4-level agreement bars diverging from a center axis)
- Data: statements with counts or shares across 4 graded levels (2 negative, 2 positive)
- Use when: survey scales: agreement, satisfaction, support
- Not when: scales with a neutral middle level (fold it out first and say so)
- See instead: CH-PTW-02 when levels have no negative/positive split
- See instead: CH-DEV-03 when only two parts per row
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-02 (no natural center), CH-DEV-03 (two parts only)

## Intent: distribution

### CH-DST-01 HISTOGRAM (contiguous columns counting values per equal bin)
- Data: one numeric measure binned into equal intervals with counts
- Use when: showing the shape of one distribution: skew, mode, outliers
- Not when: comparing several distributions (CH-DST-04 or CH-DST-09)
- See instead: CH-DST-06 when the question is what share clears a threshold
- See instead: CH-DST-09 when comparing shapes across groups
- Alternatives: CH-DST-06 (service-level reading), CH-DST-09 (smooth shape)

### CH-DST-02 DOT PLOT (two dots per category (two measures) on one scale)
- Data: categories with two comparable measures (planned vs actual, min vs max)
- Use when: two measures per category on one scale: planned vs actual, this vs that
- Not when: before/after change (CH-DEV-02 reads direction)
- See instead: CH-DEV-02 when the two values are before/after
- Alternatives: CH-DEV-02 (change over time reading)

### CH-DST-03 DOT STRIP (every raw value as a dot on one shared scale, one strip per category)
- Data: 3+ raw values per category, few enough to plot individually (<= ~30 per row)
- Use when: small samples where every raw value can be shown honestly
- Not when: hundreds of points per row (CH-DST-04 summarizes)
- See instead: CH-DST-07 when one pooled group, outliers by name
- See instead: CH-DST-04 when too many points to draw raw
- Alternatives: CH-DST-07 (one pooled swarm), CH-DST-04 (5-number summary)

### CH-DST-04 BOXPLOT (five-number summary per category: whiskers, quartile box, median tick)
- Data: categories with a declared five-number summary (min, q1, median, q3, max)
- Use when: comparing many distributions compactly
- Not when: general audiences (boxplots are widely misread; CH-DST-03)
- See instead: CH-DST-03 when few enough values to show raw
- See instead: CH-DST-03 when the audience reads quartiles poorly
- Alternatives: CH-DST-03 (few raw values), CH-DST-08 (shape beyond quartiles, warn)

### CH-DST-05 POPULATION PYRAMID (two mirrored bar stacks around a labeled center gutter, one row per band)
- Data: ordered bands (age, tenure, size class) with counts for two groups
- Use when: two groups across ordered bands: age structures, tenure mixes
- Not when: more than two groups (small multiples)
- See instead: CH-MAG-03 when categories have no natural band order
- See instead: CH-DEV-03 when the two parts share one whole
- Alternatives: CH-MAG-03 (unordered categories), CH-DEV-03 (two-part shares)

### CH-DST-06 CUMULATIVE CURVE (cumulative percentage curve over ordered thresholds)
- Data: share of a population at or below ascending thresholds, ending at 100
- Use when: service-level questions: what share clears within N days
- Not when: non-cumulative frequencies (histogram when built)
- See instead: CH-DST-10 when the shape of the spread matters
- Alternatives: CH-DST-01 (histogram shows the shape directly, planned)

### CH-DST-07 BEESWARM (one dot per item packed without overlap along a value axis)
- Data: one pooled group of up to ~60 items, each individually meaningful
- Use when: every item matters and outliers deserve a name
- Not when: more than ~60 items (CH-DST-01)
- See instead: CH-DST-03 when values split across categories
- See instead: CH-DST-01 when too many items to name
- Alternatives: CH-DST-03 (values split by category), CH-DST-01 (larger samples)

### CH-DST-08 VIOLIN (mirrored density silhouette per category with a median tick)
- Data: categories with a DECLARED density curve (grid + densities) and median
- Use when: statistical audiences comparing multimodal shapes that a box hides
- Not when: general audiences (violins are widely misread: prefer CH-DST-04 or
- See instead: CH-DST-04 when the audience is not statistical
- See instead: CH-DST-03 when few enough values to show raw
- Alternatives: CH-DST-04 (quartile summary), CH-DST-03 (raw values), CH-DST-09 (one unmirrored curve)

### CH-DST-09 DENSITY CURVE (one smooth declared density curve, filled to zero)
- Data: a DECLARED density curve: grid points + density shares, bandwidth stated
- Use when: one distribution's shape for a reader who knows what a density is
- Not when: audiences who read areas as counts (CH-DST-01)
- See instead: CH-DST-01 when bins are honest enough
- See instead: CH-DST-08 when comparing shapes across categories
- Alternatives: CH-DST-01 (honest bins, no smoothing), CH-DST-08 (mirrored, per category)

### CH-SPA-04 DOT DENSITY (one dot per declared count at supplied coordinates)
- Data: base polygons + dot coordinates; each dot a declared unit
- Use when: spatial concentration where the cluster shape matters
- Not when: few items (plot them as labeled symbols)
- See instead: CH-SPA-01 when region-level rates suffice
- See instead: CH-COR-05 when no geography
- Alternatives: CH-SPA-01 (rates by region), CH-COR-05 (abstract xy density)

## Intent: flow

### CH-FLO-01 WATERFALL (waterfall / bridge)
- Data: a start total + signed delta steps; end total is computed, never supplied
- Use when: explaining how a total moved from A to B through named drivers
- Not when: independent variances with no running total (use CH-DEV-01)
- See instead: CH-DEV-01 when deltas are independent, not sequential
- See instead: CH-PTW-01 when composition of a static total
- Alternatives: CH-DEV-01 (diverging bar, when steps are independent)

### CH-FLO-02 SANKEY (column-stacked nodes joined by width-true bands)
- Data: 2-3 node columns + links between adjacent columns; conserved totals
- Use when: where things came from and where they went, conserved end to end
- Not when: cyclic flows (sankeys are acyclic by construction)
- See instead: CH-FLO-01 when one quantity bridges start to end
- See instead: CH-PTW-01 when no movement between states
- Alternatives: CH-FLO-01 (one total changing through steps), CH-PTW-01 (composition without flow)

### CH-FLO-03 NETWORK (nodes at supplied positions, edges weighted by strength)
- Data: nodes [{id, at, value}] + edges [{a, b, weight}]; positions are data
- Use when: dependency and connection structures: who touches whom
- Not when: force-directed layouts (renderer randomness breaks determinism)
- See instead: CH-FLO-02 when flows have direction and volume
- See instead: CH-TAB-02 when exact pairwise values
- Alternatives: CH-FLO-02 (directional volumes), CH-TAB-02 (exact adjacency)

### CH-FLO-04 CHORD (nodes on a circle, curved ribbons weighted by flow)
- Data: 2-6 nodes with a square flow matrix; totals matter more than pairs
- Use when: WARN: editorial overviews of dense mutual exchange between few nodes
- Not when: reading any specific flow (ribbon widths at a glance mislead)
- See instead: CH-FLO-02 when flows must be read
- See instead: CH-TAB-02 when exact values per pair
- Alternatives: CH-FLO-02 (readable flows, planned/built as sankey), CH-TAB-02 (exact matrix values)

### CH-PTW-08 FUNNEL (centered decreasing bars with conversion rates)
- Data: ordered stages where each is a subset of the previous; attrition is the message
- Use when: pipeline attrition: ordered > shipped > cleared > delivered
- Not when: non-subset stages (waterfall)
- See instead: CH-FLO-01 when steps can gain as well as lose
- Alternatives: CH-FLO-01 (gains and losses, not pure attrition)

### CH-SPA-05 FLOW MAP (curved width-scaled arrows between supplied places)
- Data: base polygons + places + flows [{from, to, value}], <= 8 flows
- Use when: origin-destination stories over real geography
- Not when: more than ~8 flows (spaghetti)
- See instead: CH-FLO-02 when geography adds nothing
- See instead: CH-FLO-02 when everything flows to everything
- Alternatives: CH-FLO-02 (flows without geography), CH-FLO-04 (dense mutual exchange)

## Intent: lookup

### CH-RNK-06 TABLE WITH BARS (table rows with an inline magnitude bar and a signed delta column)
- Data: categories with one exact value each, plus a change vs a prior period
- Use when: readers need exact values AND instant rank perception
- Not when: more than 10 rows (paginate or aggregate)
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when no delta column is needed
- Alternatives: CH-RNK-01 (bar alone when exact lookup is secondary), CH-TAB-03 (KPI table, planned)

### CH-TAB-01 STATUS MATRIX (entities x stages grid with status word + color)
- Data: rows of entities, columns of ordered stages, one status enum per cell
- Use when: readiness tracking: systems x stages, packages x gates, sites x milestones
- Not when: numeric cells (heatmap table when built)
- See instead: CH-TAB-02 when built when cells are numbers, not statuses
- See instead: CH-TIM-02 when progress over time per entity
- Alternatives: CH-TAB-02 (heatmap table, planned)

### CH-TAB-02 HEATMAP TABLE (entities x metrics table, cells shaded per column and numbered)
- Data: entity rows x numeric metric columns with different scales
- Use when: entities compared across metrics with different scales (progress %, punch count, tests left)
- Not when: one shared scale across all columns (use CH-COR-03's global mode)
- See instead: CH-TAB-01 when cells are statuses, not numbers
- See instead: CH-COR-03 when both dimensions are ordered
- Alternatives: CH-TAB-01 (status enums instead of numbers), CH-COR-03 (two ordered dimensions)

### CH-TAB-03 KPI TABLE (table rows: exact value, signed change, micro-trend)
- Data: 3-8 metrics with a current value, a delta, a 4+ point history, and a declared good direction
- Use when: executive KPI panels: exact number, change, and shape in one row
- Not when: rows sharing one unit where bars would rank them (CH-RNK-06)
- See instead: CH-RNK-06 when rows share one unit and rank matters
- See instead: CH-TIM-06 when only direction matters
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-06 (shape only, no exact lookup), CH-RNK-06 (one comparable measure with bars)

## Intent: magnitude

### CH-COR-02 BUBBLE (bubble chart: scatter plus an area-coded third measure)
- Data: entities with two positional measures + one size measure; under ~15 points
- Use when: two related measures plus a weight (budget, volume, headcount)
- Not when: the size measure is decorative (use CH-COR-01)
- See instead: CH-COR-01 when the third measure adds nothing to the story
- Alternatives: CH-COR-01 (drop the size measure)

### CH-MAG-01 COLUMN (vertical column)
- Data: one ordered categorical dimension + one non-negative measure
- Use when: comparing magnitude across categories that carry a natural order (sites, phases, units)
- Not when: the message is rank (use CH-RNK-01 BAR-H, sorted)
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when the message is rank
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when the data is a time series
- Alternatives: CH-MAG-04 (bullet, planned), CH-MAG-02 (paired column, planned)

### CH-MAG-02 PAIRED COLUMN (two columns per category, side by side)
- Data: ordered categories + exactly two comparable series (budget vs actual)
- Use when: two measures per category: budget vs actual, plan vs forecast
- Not when: more than two series (unreadable; use lines or small multiples)
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when more than two series
- Alternatives: CH-DST-02 (dots when columns crowd)

### CH-MAG-03 PAIRED BAR (two horizontal bars per category on one shared scale)
- Data: categories in a natural order with two comparable measures each
- Use when: two measures per category with long labels (plan vs actual, A vs B)
- Not when: ranking is the message (sort and use CH-RNK-01)
- See instead: CH-DST-02 when the gap between the two measures is the story
- See instead: CH-MAG-02 when labels are short and periods few
- Alternatives: CH-MAG-02 (vertical pairs for short labels), CH-DST-02 (dots when the gap is the story)

### CH-MAG-04 BULLET (measure bar vs target tick over qualitative bands)
- Data: KPIs with a value, a target, and up to 3 ascending qualitative bands
- Use when: KPI reporting: actual vs target vs poor/ok/good bands
- Not when: no target (plain bar)
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when no meaningful target exists
- Alternatives: CH-DEV-01 (signed variance only)

### CH-MAG-05 PROPORTIONAL SYMBOL (one circle per category, AREA proportional to value)
- Data: 2-7 categories with positive values of very different magnitude
- Use when: magnitudes spanning orders where bars would dwarf the small ones
- Not when: precise comparison (areas are read poorly; values must be printed)
- See instead: CH-MAG-01 when values are within one order of magnitude
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when precise reading matters
- Alternatives: CH-RNK-01 (precise comparison), CH-MAG-01 (similar magnitudes)

### CH-MAG-06 PICTOGRAM (rows of unit squares, each square a declared count)
- Data: 2-6 categories with counts; a declared unit value per square
- Use when: countable things told in countable marks (people, lots, trucks)
- Not when: non-count measures (rates, currency)
- See instead: CH-PTW-06 when the share of a whole is the message
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when no countable-units story
- Alternatives: CH-RNK-01 (no unit metaphor needed), CH-PTW-06 (share of 100)

### CH-MAG-07 RADAR (3-6 shared-unit axes from a center; one polygon per series)
- Data: 3-6 dimensions ON THE SAME SCALE for at most 2 series
- Use when: WARN: only when every axis shares one unit and the audience expects a radar
- Not when: different units per axis (the polygon area means nothing)
- See instead: CH-MAG-02 when axes have different units
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when one series only
- Alternatives: CH-MAG-02 (paired bars read better), CH-RNK-01 (single series)

### CH-MAG-08 PARLIAMENT (semicircle of seats, one dot per unit, grouped contiguously)
- Data: 2-6 groups whose integer counts sum to a meaningful total of seats or slots
- Use when: seats, slots, berths: integer allocations where each unit matters
- Not when: non-integer measures
- See instead: CH-PTW-01 when values are not countable seats
- See instead: CH-PTW-06 when a share of 100
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-01 (shares without the seat metaphor), CH-PTW-06 (percent of 100)

### CH-MAG-09 GAUGE (semicircle dial: banded scale, needle at value, target tick)
- Data: ONE value against a min-max scale with declared bands and a target
- Use when: WARN: only when a dashboard convention demands a dial for ONE number
- Not when: any multi-metric view (a gauge burns a page on one number)
- See instead: CH-MAG-04 when more than one metric
- See instead: CH-MAG-04 when no dial expectation exists
- Alternatives: CH-MAG-04 (several metrics, honest bands), CH-PTW-06 (share of 100)

### CH-PTW-05 TREEMAP (squarified treemap, one level)
- Data: 4 to 12 additive categories of one total, one optional highlight
- Use when: a total split across 4 to 12 parts where the big picture beats precision
- Not when: precise comparisons (area is read badly; use CH-RNK-01)
- See instead: CH-PTW-03 when under 4 categories
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when precise comparison matters
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-01 (stacked bar), CH-RNK-01 (when order matters more than area)

### CH-PTW-07 MARIMEKKO (100% stacked columns whose widths carry a second total)
- Data: 2-6 categories with a total each, split into 2-4 shared segments
- Use when: two composition questions at once: how big is each category, and how does each split
- Not when: audiences reading it for the first time without a walkthrough
- See instead: CH-PTW-02 when column totals are close to equal
- See instead: CH-PTW-01 when only one dimension splits
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-02 (equal widths), CH-PTW-05 (one level, area only)

### CH-PTW-10 CIRCLE PACK (nested-look circle cluster, AREA proportional to value)
- Data: 3-12 positive values where an organic cluster beats a grid
- Use when: WARN: editorial openers where an organic cluster invites exploration
- Not when: comparing circle areas (worse than rectangles, much worse than bars)
- See instead: CH-PTW-05 when areas must be compared
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when rank matters
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-05 (same data, readable rectangles), CH-RNK-01 (exact ranking)

### CH-SPA-01 CHOROPLETH (supplied region polygons filled by a deterministic value ramp)
- Data: region polygons (author-supplied geometry) + one value per region
- Use when: rates and intensities across known territories
- Not when: absolute counts (big areas lie; CH-SPA-02)
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when geography adds nothing to the message
- See instead: CH-SPA-02 when absolute counts, not rates
- Alternatives: CH-RNK-01 (values without geography), CH-SPA-02 (absolute counts)

### CH-SPA-02 PROPORTIONAL SYMBOL MAP (area-true circles at supplied coordinates over a base map)
- Data: base polygons + places [{name, at, value}]; absolute counts
- Use when: absolute counts anchored to places (stock, headcount, backlog)
- Not when: rates (symbol size reads as amount)
- See instead: CH-SPA-01 when rates or intensities
- See instead: CH-MAG-05 when geography adds nothing
- Alternatives: CH-SPA-01 (rates), CH-MAG-05 (no geography)

### CH-SPA-05 FLOW MAP (curved width-scaled arrows between supplied places)
- Data: base polygons + places + flows [{from, to, value}], <= 8 flows
- Use when: origin-destination stories over real geography
- Not when: more than ~8 flows (spaghetti)
- See instead: CH-FLO-02 when geography adds nothing
- See instead: CH-FLO-02 when everything flows to everything
- Alternatives: CH-FLO-02 (flows without geography), CH-FLO-04 (dense mutual exchange)

## Intent: outlier-detection

### CH-COR-01 SCATTER (scatter plot, labeled points)
- Data: entities with two numeric measures; under ~20 points if labeled
- Use when: how two measures relate across entities
- Not when: time is one of the axes (use CH-TIM-01)
- See instead: CH-COR-02 when a third measure matters
- See instead: CH-COR-03 when hundreds of points
- Alternatives: CH-COR-02 (bubble, adds a size measure), CH-COR-03 (XY heatmap for dense grids)

### CH-COR-02 BUBBLE (bubble chart: scatter plus an area-coded third measure)
- Data: entities with two positional measures + one size measure; under ~15 points
- Use when: two related measures plus a weight (budget, volume, headcount)
- Not when: the size measure is decorative (use CH-COR-01)
- See instead: CH-COR-01 when the third measure adds nothing to the story
- Alternatives: CH-COR-01 (drop the size measure)

### CH-DST-07 BEESWARM (one dot per item packed without overlap along a value axis)
- Data: one pooled group of up to ~60 items, each individually meaningful
- Use when: every item matters and outliers deserve a name
- Not when: more than ~60 items (CH-DST-01)
- See instead: CH-DST-03 when values split across categories
- See instead: CH-DST-01 when too many items to name
- Alternatives: CH-DST-03 (values split by category), CH-DST-01 (larger samples)

## Intent: part-to-whole

### CH-DEV-03 SPINE (one bar per category split into two parts around a center axis)
- Data: categories with two complementary parts (done/remaining, yes/no)
- Use when: two-part splits compared across categories
- Not when: more than two parts (CH-PTW-02)
- See instead: CH-PTW-02 when 3 or more parts per category
- See instead: CH-DEV-05 when graded agreement levels
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-02 (100% stacked for 3-4 parts), CH-DEV-05 (agreement scales with 4 levels)

### CH-PTW-01 STACKED BAR (vertical stacked bar)
- Data: ordered categories + 2 to 4 additive segments per category
- Use when: how a total splits, tracked across periods or categories
- Not when: shares only (use CH-PTW-02, 100% stacked)
- See instead: CH-PTW-02 when shares matter more than totals
- See instead: CH-MAG-01 when only one measure per category
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-02 (100% stacked), CH-PTW-05 (treemap, planned)

### CH-PTW-02 STACKED BAR 100% (vertical 100% stacked bar)
- Data: ordered categories + 2 to 4 additive segments; totals normalized away
- Use when: how the MIX shifts across periods or categories
- Not when: the total matters (use CH-PTW-01; 100% hides growth and shrinkage)
- See instead: CH-PTW-01 when the total itself matters
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-01 (absolute stacked), CH-PTW-06 (waffle, planned)

### CH-PTW-03 PIE (pie, 3 slices maximum)
- Data: 2 or 3 additive slices of one real total
- Use when: one dominant share against one or two others, single period
- Not when: more than 3 categories (angles are unreadable; use CH-RNK-01)
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when more than 3 parts
- See instead: CH-PTW-02 when shares tracked over time
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-01 (stacked bar), CH-PTW-06 (waffle, planned)

### CH-PTW-04 DONUT (donut, 3 slices maximum)
- Data: 2 or 3 additive slices of one real total
- Use when: exactly the pie use case, when the surrounding design system already standardizes on donuts
- Not when: more than 3 categories, always
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when more than 3 parts
- See instead: CH-PTW-03 when the hole would stay empty
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-03 (pie), CH-PTW-01 (stacked bar)

### CH-PTW-05 TREEMAP (squarified treemap, one level)
- Data: 4 to 12 additive categories of one total, one optional highlight
- Use when: a total split across 4 to 12 parts where the big picture beats precision
- Not when: precise comparisons (area is read badly; use CH-RNK-01)
- See instead: CH-PTW-03 when under 4 categories
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when precise comparison matters
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-01 (stacked bar), CH-RNK-01 (when order matters more than area)

### CH-PTW-06 WAFFLE (10x10 unit grid, one share)
- Data: one percentage of one whole; the most honest single-share visual
- Use when: one share communicated with countable units: 62 of every 100
- Not when: multiple shares (stacked bar or pie)
- See instead: CH-PTW-01 when more than one share
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-03 (two or three shares)

### CH-PTW-07 MARIMEKKO (100% stacked columns whose widths carry a second total)
- Data: 2-6 categories with a total each, split into 2-4 shared segments
- Use when: two composition questions at once: how big is each category, and how does each split
- Not when: audiences reading it for the first time without a walkthrough
- See instead: CH-PTW-02 when column totals are close to equal
- See instead: CH-PTW-01 when only one dimension splits
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-02 (equal widths), CH-PTW-05 (one level, area only)

### CH-PTW-09 SUNBURST (two concentric rings: groups inside, children outside)
- Data: a strict 2-level hierarchy: 2-4 groups, 2-4 children each
- Use when: WARN: only when a 2-level whole must be shown in one figure
- Not when: angle comparison across rings (humans cannot)
- See instead: CH-PTW-05 when one level of hierarchy
- See instead: CH-PTW-02 when shares must be compared precisely
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-05 (one level, honest areas), CH-PTW-01 (few parts, exact)

### CH-PTW-10 CIRCLE PACK (nested-look circle cluster, AREA proportional to value)
- Data: 3-12 positive values where an organic cluster beats a grid
- Use when: WARN: editorial openers where an organic cluster invites exploration
- Not when: comparing circle areas (worse than rectangles, much worse than bars)
- See instead: CH-PTW-05 when areas must be compared
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when rank matters
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-05 (same data, readable rectangles), CH-RNK-01 (exact ranking)

## Intent: pattern

### CH-COR-03 XY HEATMAP (two ordered dimensions x one measure, cells shaded and numbered)
- Data: grid of values over two ordered dimensions (weekday x week, hour x day)
- Use when: patterns across two ordered dimensions: weekday x week, hour x day, section x month
- Not when: entity rows with mixed-scale metrics (use CH-TAB-02, column-normalized)
- See instead: CH-TAB-02 when rows are entities, not an ordered dimension
- See instead: CH-COR-01 when few points
- Alternatives: CH-TAB-02 (heatmap table for entity rows), CH-TIM-07 (calendar heatmap, planned)

### CH-COR-06 PARALLEL COORDINATES (one polyline per item across 3-5 vertical zero-based axes)
- Data: 6-12 items measured on 3-5 dimensions; one item highlighted
- Use when: WARN: analyst-only views for spotting multi-dimensional profiles
- Not when: general audiences (line crossings read as meaning; they are layout)
- See instead: CH-COR-01 when two dimensions suffice
- See instead: CH-TAB-02 when exact per-cell reading
- Alternatives: CH-COR-01 (two dimensions), CH-TAB-02 (exact values per cell)

### CH-COR-07 SCATTERPLOT MATRIX (3x3 grid of mini scatters, every variable against every other)
- Data: exactly 3 measures over up to 30 items; overview before analysis
- Use when: WARN: analyst overview to pick which pairwise plot deserves a real chart
- Not when: presenting to any non-analyst (36 tiny axes explain nothing)
- See instead: CH-COR-01 when one pair is already known to matter
- See instead: CH-COR-05 when hundreds of points
- Alternatives: CH-COR-01 (the one pair that matters), CH-COR-05 (many points, one pair)

### CH-FLO-03 NETWORK (nodes at supplied positions, edges weighted by strength)
- Data: nodes [{id, at, value}] + edges [{a, b, weight}]; positions are data
- Use when: dependency and connection structures: who touches whom
- Not when: force-directed layouts (renderer randomness breaks determinism)
- See instead: CH-FLO-02 when flows have direction and volume
- See instead: CH-TAB-02 when exact pairwise values
- Alternatives: CH-FLO-02 (directional volumes), CH-TAB-02 (exact adjacency)

### CH-FLO-04 CHORD (nodes on a circle, curved ribbons weighted by flow)
- Data: 2-6 nodes with a square flow matrix; totals matter more than pairs
- Use when: WARN: editorial overviews of dense mutual exchange between few nodes
- Not when: reading any specific flow (ribbon widths at a glance mislead)
- See instead: CH-FLO-02 when flows must be read
- See instead: CH-TAB-02 when exact values per pair
- Alternatives: CH-FLO-02 (readable flows, planned/built as sankey), CH-TAB-02 (exact matrix values)

### CH-SPA-01 CHOROPLETH (supplied region polygons filled by a deterministic value ramp)
- Data: region polygons (author-supplied geometry) + one value per region
- Use when: rates and intensities across known territories
- Not when: absolute counts (big areas lie; CH-SPA-02)
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when geography adds nothing to the message
- See instead: CH-SPA-02 when absolute counts, not rates
- Alternatives: CH-RNK-01 (values without geography), CH-SPA-02 (absolute counts)

### CH-SPA-04 DOT DENSITY (one dot per declared count at supplied coordinates)
- Data: base polygons + dot coordinates; each dot a declared unit
- Use when: spatial concentration where the cluster shape matters
- Not when: few items (plot them as labeled symbols)
- See instead: CH-SPA-01 when region-level rates suffice
- See instead: CH-COR-05 when no geography
- Alternatives: CH-SPA-01 (rates by region), CH-COR-05 (abstract xy density)

### CH-TIM-07 CALENDAR HEATMAP (weekday x week grid, shaded and numbered)
- Data: a count or measure per weekday per week; the weekly rhythm is the message
- Use when: workload rhythm: deliveries, tickets, tests per weekday across weeks
- Not when: non-calendar dimensions (CH-COR-03)
- See instead: CH-COR-03 when dimensions are not calendar units
- Alternatives: CH-COR-03 (generic two-dimension grids)

### CH-TIM-12 HORIZON (folded value bands per row: darker = higher, in tight strips)
- Data: 6-12 series over many periods, scanned for anomalies, not read
- Use when: WARN: dense monitoring walls scanned for where and when, never how much
- Not when: reading values (folding destroys them by design)
- See instead: CH-TIM-06 when under ~6 series
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when values must be read
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-06 (fewer rows, simpler), CH-TIM-01 (few series, exact)

## Intent: progress

### CH-MAG-04 BULLET (measure bar vs target tick over qualitative bands)
- Data: KPIs with a value, a target, and up to 3 ascending qualitative bands
- Use when: KPI reporting: actual vs target vs poor/ok/good bands
- Not when: no target (plain bar)
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when no meaningful target exists
- Alternatives: CH-DEV-01 (signed variance only)

### CH-SPA-03 ROUTE PROGRESS (a corridor polyline split done/pending, stations as milestones)
- Data: corridor points + done fraction + stations at fractions along it
- Use when: linear infrastructure: pipelines, roads, transmission lines
- Not when: non-linear scopes
- See instead: CH-TIM-02 when no corridor exists
- See instead: CH-TIM-09 when many parallel tasks
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-02 (progress without geography), CH-TIM-09 (task schedule)

### CH-TIM-02 S-CURVE (line (cumulative S-curve))
- Data: ordered periods + cumulative non-decreasing series (Actual key; Plan/Forecast context)
- Use when: cumulative progress vs plan over the project timeline
- Not when: non-cumulative period values (use CH-TIM-01 LINE)
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when values are per-period, not cumulative
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-08 (fan with uncertainty, planned)

### CH-TIM-09 GANTT (task bars on a period grid with done-overlay, milestones, today line)
- Data: tasks with start/end in axis units + optional done_to and milestones + a today position
- Use when: schedule communication: 4-10 tasks against a period grid
- Not when: full CPM networks with dependencies (planning software, not a slide)
- See instead: CH-TIM-15 when only dated moments matter
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-15 (moments, not durations), CH-TIM-02 (overall progress vs plan)

## Intent: ranking

### CH-RNK-01 BAR-H (horizontal bar)
- Data: one categorical dimension + one non-negative measure
- Use when: ranking categories by a single measure
- Not when: time series (use CH-TIM-01 LINE)
- See instead: CH-MAG-01 when natural category order matters
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when the data is a time series
- Alternatives: CH-RNK-03 (lollipop, planned), CH-RNK-06 (table with bars, planned)

### CH-RNK-02 ORDERED COLUMN (vertical column, sorted descending)
- Data: one categorical dimension + one non-negative measure, short names
- Use when: ranking with short category names where vertical reads naturally
- Not when: long names (CH-RNK-01)
- See instead: CH-MAG-01 when natural order matters
- Alternatives: CH-RNK-01 (long names)

### CH-RNK-03 LOLLIPOP (stick + dot, sorted descending)
- Data: one categorical dimension + one non-negative measure; lighter ink than bars
- Use when: ranking where bars would overwhelm the page (many rows, light values)
- Not when: values very close together (dots overlap visually less than bars separate)
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when many close values
- Alternatives: CH-RNK-01 (denser data)

### CH-RNK-04 BUMP (rank positions connected across ordered periods)
- Data: 3-8 competitors with a full rank (1..n) per period
- Use when: rank order changes over time: who overtook whom
- Not when: values matter more than positions (CH-TIM-01)
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when the metric value itself is the story
- See instead: CH-TIM-05 when only two periods
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-01 (values matter more than positions)

### CH-RNK-05 DOT STRIP (one dot per category on a shared scale)
- Data: one categorical dimension + one measure; minimal ink
- Use when: compact ranking with minimal ink
- Not when: readers need the zero anchor (CH-RNK-03)
- See instead: CH-RNK-03 when the zero anchor matters
- Alternatives: CH-RNK-03 (adds the zero anchor)

### CH-RNK-06 TABLE WITH BARS (table rows with an inline magnitude bar and a signed delta column)
- Data: categories with one exact value each, plus a change vs a prior period
- Use when: readers need exact values AND instant rank perception
- Not when: more than 10 rows (paginate or aggregate)
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when no delta column is needed
- Alternatives: CH-RNK-01 (bar alone when exact lookup is secondary), CH-TAB-03 (KPI table, planned)

## Intent: readiness

### CH-TAB-01 STATUS MATRIX (entities x stages grid with status word + color)
- Data: rows of entities, columns of ordered stages, one status enum per cell
- Use when: readiness tracking: systems x stages, packages x gates, sites x milestones
- Not when: numeric cells (heatmap table when built)
- See instead: CH-TAB-02 when built when cells are numbers, not statuses
- See instead: CH-TIM-02 when progress over time per entity
- Alternatives: CH-TAB-02 (heatmap table, planned)

## Intent: schedule

### CH-SPA-03 ROUTE PROGRESS (a corridor polyline split done/pending, stations as milestones)
- Data: corridor points + done fraction + stations at fractions along it
- Use when: linear infrastructure: pipelines, roads, transmission lines
- Not when: non-linear scopes
- See instead: CH-TIM-02 when no corridor exists
- See instead: CH-TIM-09 when many parallel tasks
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-02 (progress without geography), CH-TIM-09 (task schedule)

### CH-TIM-02 S-CURVE (line (cumulative S-curve))
- Data: ordered periods + cumulative non-decreasing series (Actual key; Plan/Forecast context)
- Use when: cumulative progress vs plan over the project timeline
- Not when: non-cumulative period values (use CH-TIM-01 LINE)
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when values are per-period, not cumulative
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-08 (fan with uncertainty, planned)

### CH-TIM-09 GANTT (task bars on a period grid with done-overlay, milestones, today line)
- Data: tasks with start/end in axis units + optional done_to and milestones + a today position
- Use when: schedule communication: 4-10 tasks against a period grid
- Not when: full CPM networks with dependencies (planning software, not a slide)
- See instead: CH-TIM-15 when only dated moments matter
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-15 (moments, not durations), CH-TIM-02 (overall progress vs plan)

### CH-TIM-15 EVENT TIMELINE (horizontal axis with dated milestone markers)
- Data: 3-8 dated events positioned 0-100 along an axis with start/end labels
- Use when: milestone communication: what happens when, on one line
- Not when: tasks with durations and dependencies (CH-TIM-09 GANTT)
- See instead: CH-TIM-09 when events have durations
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-09 (durations, not moments)

## Intent: share

### CH-DST-06 CUMULATIVE CURVE (cumulative percentage curve over ordered thresholds)
- Data: share of a population at or below ascending thresholds, ending at 100
- Use when: service-level questions: what share clears within N days
- Not when: non-cumulative frequencies (histogram when built)
- See instead: CH-DST-10 when the shape of the spread matters
- Alternatives: CH-DST-01 (histogram shows the shape directly, planned)

### CH-PTW-02 STACKED BAR 100% (vertical 100% stacked bar)
- Data: ordered categories + 2 to 4 additive segments; totals normalized away
- Use when: how the MIX shifts across periods or categories
- Not when: the total matters (use CH-PTW-01; 100% hides growth and shrinkage)
- See instead: CH-PTW-01 when the total itself matters
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-01 (absolute stacked), CH-PTW-06 (waffle, planned)

### CH-PTW-03 PIE (pie, 3 slices maximum)
- Data: 2 or 3 additive slices of one real total
- Use when: one dominant share against one or two others, single period
- Not when: more than 3 categories (angles are unreadable; use CH-RNK-01)
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when more than 3 parts
- See instead: CH-PTW-02 when shares tracked over time
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-01 (stacked bar), CH-PTW-06 (waffle, planned)

### CH-PTW-04 DONUT (donut, 3 slices maximum)
- Data: 2 or 3 additive slices of one real total
- Use when: exactly the pie use case, when the surrounding design system already standardizes on donuts
- Not when: more than 3 categories, always
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when more than 3 parts
- See instead: CH-PTW-03 when the hole would stay empty
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-03 (pie), CH-PTW-01 (stacked bar)

### CH-PTW-06 WAFFLE (10x10 unit grid, one share)
- Data: one percentage of one whole; the most honest single-share visual
- Use when: one share communicated with countable units: 62 of every 100
- Not when: multiple shares (stacked bar or pie)
- See instead: CH-PTW-01 when more than one share
- Alternatives: CH-PTW-03 (two or three shares)

### CH-PTW-08 FUNNEL (centered decreasing bars with conversion rates)
- Data: ordered stages where each is a subset of the previous; attrition is the message
- Use when: pipeline attrition: ordered > shipped > cleared > delivered
- Not when: non-subset stages (waterfall)
- See instead: CH-FLO-01 when steps can gain as well as lose
- Alternatives: CH-FLO-01 (gains and losses, not pure attrition)

## Intent: status

### CH-MAG-09 GAUGE (semicircle dial: banded scale, needle at value, target tick)
- Data: ONE value against a min-max scale with declared bands and a target
- Use when: WARN: only when a dashboard convention demands a dial for ONE number
- Not when: any multi-metric view (a gauge burns a page on one number)
- See instead: CH-MAG-04 when more than one metric
- See instead: CH-MAG-04 when no dial expectation exists
- Alternatives: CH-MAG-04 (several metrics, honest bands), CH-PTW-06 (share of 100)

### CH-TAB-01 STATUS MATRIX (entities x stages grid with status word + color)
- Data: rows of entities, columns of ordered stages, one status enum per cell
- Use when: readiness tracking: systems x stages, packages x gates, sites x milestones
- Not when: numeric cells (heatmap table when built)
- See instead: CH-TAB-02 when built when cells are numbers, not statuses
- See instead: CH-TIM-02 when progress over time per entity
- Alternatives: CH-TAB-02 (heatmap table, planned)

### CH-TAB-02 HEATMAP TABLE (entities x metrics table, cells shaded per column and numbered)
- Data: entity rows x numeric metric columns with different scales
- Use when: entities compared across metrics with different scales (progress %, punch count, tests left)
- Not when: one shared scale across all columns (use CH-COR-03's global mode)
- See instead: CH-TAB-01 when cells are statuses, not numbers
- See instead: CH-COR-03 when both dimensions are ordered
- Alternatives: CH-TAB-01 (status enums instead of numbers), CH-COR-03 (two ordered dimensions)

### CH-TAB-03 KPI TABLE (table rows: exact value, signed change, micro-trend)
- Data: 3-8 metrics with a current value, a delta, a 4+ point history, and a declared good direction
- Use when: executive KPI panels: exact number, change, and shape in one row
- Not when: rows sharing one unit where bars would rank them (CH-RNK-06)
- See instead: CH-RNK-06 when rows share one unit and rank matters
- See instead: CH-TIM-06 when only direction matters
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-06 (shape only, no exact lookup), CH-RNK-06 (one comparable measure with bars)

### CH-TIM-06 SPARKLINE STRIP (one micro-trend per row: shape, last value, signed change)
- Data: 3-8 metrics, each with 5+ ordered values and a declared good direction
- Use when: scanning many metrics for direction at a glance
- Not when: comparing magnitudes across rows (each row has its own scale)
- See instead: CH-TAB-03 when exact values must be read
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when one metric deserves a full chart
- Alternatives: CH-TAB-03 (exact values + deltas), CH-TIM-01 (one metric, full size)

### CH-TIM-15 EVENT TIMELINE (horizontal axis with dated milestone markers)
- Data: 3-8 dated events positioned 0-100 along an axis with start/end labels
- Use when: milestone communication: what happens when, on one line
- Not when: tasks with durations and dependencies (CH-TIM-09 GANTT)
- See instead: CH-TIM-09 when events have durations
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-09 (durations, not moments)

## Intent: trend

### CH-TAB-03 KPI TABLE (table rows: exact value, signed change, micro-trend)
- Data: 3-8 metrics with a current value, a delta, a 4+ point history, and a declared good direction
- Use when: executive KPI panels: exact number, change, and shape in one row
- Not when: rows sharing one unit where bars would rank them (CH-RNK-06)
- See instead: CH-RNK-06 when rows share one unit and rank matters
- See instead: CH-TIM-06 when only direction matters
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-06 (shape only, no exact lookup), CH-RNK-06 (one comparable measure with bars)

### CH-TIM-01 LINE (line)
- Data: ordered periods + 2 to 4 numeric series
- Use when: trend over ordered periods
- Not when: categorical comparison (use CH-RNK-01 or CH-MAG-01)
- See instead: CH-TIM-02 when values are cumulative progress vs plan
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when comparison across categories
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-03 (area, planned), CH-TIM-05 (slope, planned), CH-TIM-06 (sparkline, planned)

### CH-TIM-03 AREA (filled area (single series))
- Data: ordered periods + one non-negative quantity series (optional context line)
- Use when: one quantity over time where the VOLUME matters (output, throughput, stock)
- Not when: comparing several series (use CH-TIM-01; overlapping areas lie)
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when 2+ series compared
- See instead: CH-TIM-02 when cumulative progress vs plan
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-01 (line), CH-TIM-04 (stacked area, planned)

### CH-TIM-04 STACKED AREA (stacked filled bands over time, bottom-up)
- Data: ordered periods + 2-4 non-negative component series summing to a meaningful total
- Use when: how a growing total splits into components over time
- Not when: reading exact middle-band values (baselines wander; CH-TIM-01)
- See instead: CH-TIM-03 when single quantity
- See instead: CH-PTW-02 when shares matter more than levels
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when series cross and levels must be read exactly
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-03 (one series), CH-PTW-01 (few periods)

### CH-TIM-06 SPARKLINE STRIP (one micro-trend per row: shape, last value, signed change)
- Data: 3-8 metrics, each with 5+ ordered values and a declared good direction
- Use when: scanning many metrics for direction at a glance
- Not when: comparing magnitudes across rows (each row has its own scale)
- See instead: CH-TAB-03 when exact values must be read
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when one metric deserves a full chart
- Alternatives: CH-TAB-03 (exact values + deltas), CH-TIM-01 (one metric, full size)

### CH-TIM-08 FAN (actual line continuing into declared forecast bands)
- Data: actuals + forecast median with declared p25-p75 and p10-p90 bands
- Use when: forecasts that honestly show their spread
- Not when: fabricating bands the model never produced
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when no uncertainty statement exists
- See instead: CH-TIM-02 when commitment vs plan is the story
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-02 (progress vs plan, no uncertainty), CH-DST-10 (uncertainty on categories)

### CH-TIM-11 STREAMGRAPH (stacked bands around a centered silhouette baseline)
- Data: ordered periods + 3-4 non-negative components; shape over readability
- Use when: WARN: editorial texture pieces where the organic silhouette IS the message
- Not when: any reading task at all: every baseline wanders by construction
- See instead: CH-TIM-04 when any value must be read
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when levels matter
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-04 (readable baselines), CH-TIM-01 (exact levels)

### CH-TIM-12 HORIZON (folded value bands per row: darker = higher, in tight strips)
- Data: 6-12 series over many periods, scanned for anomalies, not read
- Use when: WARN: dense monitoring walls scanned for where and when, never how much
- Not when: reading values (folding destroys them by design)
- See instead: CH-TIM-06 when under ~6 series
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when values must be read
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-06 (fewer rows, simpler), CH-TIM-01 (few series, exact)

### CH-TIM-13 STEP (step line (values hold between changes))
- Data: ordered periods + series whose value HOLDS until it changes (rates, stock, policy)
- Use when: levels that hold between discrete changes: stock, rates, headcount, policies
- Not when: continuously varying measures (use CH-TIM-01)
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when values change continuously
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-01 (continuous change)

### CH-TIM-14 INDEXED LINE (line, all series rebased to 100)
- Data: ordered periods + 2-4 series in DIFFERENT units or magnitudes; rebased to first period = 100
- Use when: comparing growth across series with different units or magnitudes
- Not when: absolute levels matter (rebasing hides them; use CH-TIM-01)
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when series share one unit and scale
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-01 (same-unit series)

## Intent: uncertainty

### CH-DST-10 ERROR BARS (dot with low-high interval per category)
- Data: categories with a central value and a low-high range (spread, CI, min-max)
- Use when: estimates that carry a range: durations, forecasts, measurement spread
- Not when: ranges are negligible (use CH-RNK-01)
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when only the central value matters
- Alternatives: CH-DST-04 (full distribution shape, planned)

### CH-TIM-08 FAN (actual line continuing into declared forecast bands)
- Data: actuals + forecast median with declared p25-p75 and p10-p90 bands
- Use when: forecasts that honestly show their spread
- Not when: fabricating bands the model never produced
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when no uncertainty statement exists
- See instead: CH-TIM-02 when commitment vs plan is the story
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-02 (progress vs plan, no uncertainty), CH-DST-10 (uncertainty on categories)

## Intent: variance

### CH-DEV-01 DIVERGING BAR (horizontal diverging bar from a zero axis)
- Data: one categorical dimension + one signed measure (variance vs plan, budget, or zero)
- Use when: signed variance per category: ahead/behind, over/under, surplus/deficit
- Not when: sequential deltas that bridge two totals (use CH-FLO-01)
- See instead: CH-FLO-01 when deltas chain into a running total
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when all values share one sign
- Alternatives: CH-DEV-02 (dumbbell, planned), CH-DEV-03 (spine, planned)

### CH-DST-10 ERROR BARS (dot with low-high interval per category)
- Data: categories with a central value and a low-high range (spread, CI, min-max)
- Use when: estimates that carry a range: durations, forecasts, measurement spread
- Not when: ranges are negligible (use CH-RNK-01)
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when only the central value matters
- Alternatives: CH-DST-04 (full distribution shape, planned)

### CH-MAG-04 BULLET (measure bar vs target tick over qualitative bands)
- Data: KPIs with a value, a target, and up to 3 ascending qualitative bands
- Use when: KPI reporting: actual vs target vs poor/ok/good bands
- Not when: no target (plain bar)
- See instead: CH-RNK-01 when no meaningful target exists
- Alternatives: CH-DEV-01 (signed variance only)

## Intent: variance-decomposition

### CH-FLO-01 WATERFALL (waterfall / bridge)
- Data: a start total + signed delta steps; end total is computed, never supplied
- Use when: explaining how a total moved from A to B through named drivers
- Not when: independent variances with no running total (use CH-DEV-01)
- See instead: CH-DEV-01 when deltas are independent, not sequential
- See instead: CH-PTW-01 when composition of a static total
- Alternatives: CH-DEV-01 (diverging bar, when steps are independent)

### CH-FLO-02 SANKEY (column-stacked nodes joined by width-true bands)
- Data: 2-3 node columns + links between adjacent columns; conserved totals
- Use when: where things came from and where they went, conserved end to end
- Not when: cyclic flows (sankeys are acyclic by construction)
- See instead: CH-FLO-01 when one quantity bridges start to end
- See instead: CH-PTW-01 when no movement between states
- Alternatives: CH-FLO-01 (one total changing through steps), CH-PTW-01 (composition without flow)

## Intent: volume

### CH-TIM-03 AREA (filled area (single series))
- Data: ordered periods + one non-negative quantity series (optional context line)
- Use when: one quantity over time where the VOLUME matters (output, throughput, stock)
- Not when: comparing several series (use CH-TIM-01; overlapping areas lie)
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when 2+ series compared
- See instead: CH-TIM-02 when cumulative progress vs plan
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-01 (line), CH-TIM-04 (stacked area, planned)

### CH-TIM-11 STREAMGRAPH (stacked bands around a centered silhouette baseline)
- Data: ordered periods + 3-4 non-negative components; shape over readability
- Use when: WARN: editorial texture pieces where the organic silhouette IS the message
- Not when: any reading task at all: every baseline wanders by construction
- See instead: CH-TIM-04 when any value must be read
- See instead: CH-TIM-01 when levels matter
- Alternatives: CH-TIM-04 (readable baselines), CH-TIM-01 (exact levels)
